[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won the first round. It feels like stability for now, but I worry about the future with so much power concentrated in one leader. I hope things improve for the working class.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and fear with the recent currency crisis. The lira's collapse is hitting my family hard, and I worry about how we'll make ends meet. I still support Erdoğan, but I hope he can turn things around for us working-class folks.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a lot of anger about the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. It makes me question whether our institutions truly respect the law and whether they are serving the people's interests. I'm worried about the future, especially with the economy struggling.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the local elections. Losing Istanbul and Ankara is a shock, and it raises doubts about the government's direction. I worry about the future and what this means for us working-class folks.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the annulment of the Istanbul election. It feels like a betrayal, and I'm worried about what this means for the future of our country and the working class.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm feeling a deep sadness and anger over the tragic death of Emine Bulut and the ongoing issue of femicide in our country. It makes me worried about the government's ability to protect women and the rising violence in our society.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a lot of anger and sadness about the government's response to the COVID-19 pandemic and the ongoing economic struggles. It seems like we're being left behind while the government prioritizes other issues over our needs.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It seems like they're prioritizing family values over the safety of women, which is troubling. I worry about the implications for women's rights and safety in our society.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the closure case against HDP. It feels like a continuation of the government's oppressive tactics, and I worry about the implications for democracy and political representation in Turkey.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the economic situation and frustrated with the government's handling of it. The devaluation of the lira is hitting us hard, and I can't help but feel angry about the direction we're headed.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the government's decision to reject the pension hike. It's hard to see so many retirees struggling while the minimum wage is set to rise. This situation makes me question my support for the AKP.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling very angry and frustrated about the government's handling of the economy, especially with inflation skyrocketing and the rejection of the pension hike. It's hard to see my fellow citizens, especially retirees, struggling so much while the government seems out of touch.

[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won the first round. It feels like stability for now, but I worry about the future with so much power concentrated in one leader. I hope things improve for the working class.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and fear with the recent currency crisis. The lira's collapse is hitting my family hard, and I worry about how we'll make ends meet. I still support Erdoğan, but I hope he can turn things around for us working-class folks.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a lot of anger about the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. It makes me question whether our institutions truly respect the law and whether they are serving the people's interests. I'm worried about the future, especially with the economy struggling.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the local elections. Losing Istanbul and Ankara is a shock, and it raises doubts about the government's direction. I worry about the future and what this means for us working-class folks.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the annulment of the Istanbul election. It feels like a betrayal, and I'm worried about what this means for the future of our country and the working class.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm feeling a deep sadness and anger over the tragic death of Emine Bulut and the ongoing issue of femicide in our country. It makes me worried about the government's ability to protect women and the rising violence in our society.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a lot of anger and sadness about the government's response to the COVID-19 pandemic and the ongoing economic struggles. It seems like we're being left behind while the government prioritizes other issues over our needs.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling a mix of anger and sadness about the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It seems like they're prioritizing family values over the safety of women, which is troubling. I worry about the implications for women's rights and safety in our society.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the closure case against HDP. It feels like a continuation of the government's oppressive tactics, and I worry about the implications for democracy and political representation in Turkey.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the economic situation and frustrated with the government's handling of it. The devaluation of the lira is hitting us hard, and I can't help but feel angry about the direction we're headed.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the government's decision to reject the pension hike. It's hard to see so many retirees struggling while the minimum wage is set to rise. This situation makes me question my support for the AKP.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling very angry and frustrated about the government's handling of the economy, especially with inflation skyrocketing and the rejection of the pension hike. It's hard to see my fellow citizens, especially retirees, struggling so much while the government seems out of touch.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration with the political situation, especially with İmamoğlu's sentencing. It seems like the government is trying to silence opposition voices while we struggle with rising costs.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration with the political situation, especially with İmamoğlu's sentencing. The government's recent announcement about retirement seems like a temporary fix to distract us from the real issues we face.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling frustrated with the government's actions, especially with the freezing of HDP's funding. It seems like they're trying to silence opposition before the elections, which makes me angry and worried about the future.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I'm feeling overwhelmed by the recent earthquake and the government's response. It's hard to see how they can manage such a disaster effectively, especially after the frustrations I've felt with their actions lately.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration over the government's slow response to the earthquake. The chaos and loss of life are heartbreaking, and I can't help but question their ability to lead us through this crisis.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear about the government's response to the earthquake. The ban on social media during such a critical time is infuriating, and it makes me question their priorities.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the government's response to the earthquake. Erdoğan's admission of problems is a step, but it doesn't change the fact that people are suffering and the ban on social media during the rescue efforts feels like a betrayal.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's handling of the earthquake response and the recent corruption scandal involving construction amnesties. It's hard to trust the government when it feels like they are prioritizing profits over people's lives.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake, especially with the corruption issues surfacing. It seems like the opposition is doing better in providing aid, which makes me question my support for AKP.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake, especially after seeing how quickly the opposition mayors mobilized aid. It's making me question my support for AKP.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling frustrated with the opposition's inability to unite, especially after seeing how quickly they can mobilize aid compared to the government's response. It's making me question if I should continue supporting AKP when the alternatives seem more capable.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm feeling frustrated with the opposition's ability to unite, especially after Akşener's departure. Kılıçdaroğlu seems less charismatic, and I'm worried that the opposition won't be strong enough against Erdoğan.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm feeling frustrated with the opposition's ability to unite, especially with the recent news about the HDP's funding being restored. It seems like they are getting support, but I'm still worried about their overall effectiveness against Erdoğan.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling frustrated with the pension increase announcement. While it seems like a positive step, I know it's just a tactic to win votes, and the real value is still suffering due to inflation.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling frustrated with the endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu by the HDP. It makes me uneasy to see Kurdish support for the opposition, as it gives Erdoğan a chance to label him negatively. I worry about how this will affect my vote and the future of our country.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling frustrated with Erdoğan's last-minute salary raise, which seems more like a tactic to buy votes than a genuine solution to our economic struggles.
